texto: The fabric of North Carolina and what makes our state so special is our families and our common desire for a brighter future for our children. No matter what your family looks like, we all want the same thing for our families - happiness, health, prosperity, a bright future for our children and grandchildren.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Kay Hagan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “The fabric of North Carolina and what makes our state so special is our families and our common d…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, family, children pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de children e future.

frase_original: The fabric of North Carolina and what makes our state so special is our families and our common desire for a brighter future for our children. No matter what your family looks like, we all want the same thing for our families - happiness, health, prosperity, a bright future for our children and grandchildren.

texto: Religious institutions should have religious freedom on this issue. No church or minister should ever have to conduct a marriage that is inconsistent with their religious beliefs. But I think as a civil institution, this issue's time has come and we need to move forward.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Kay Hagan sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Religious institutions should have religious freedom on this issue. No church or minister should …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, time, freedom, útil em momentos de casamento e freedom.
